informal: If $G_1$ and $G_2$ are cyclic groups of orders $m$ and $n$, respectively, prove that $G_1 \times G_2$ is cyclic if and only if $m$ and $n$ are relatively prime.
lean:
theorem Herstein_exercise_2_9_2 {G H : Type*} [Fintype G] [Fintype H] [Group G]
  [Group H] (hG : IsCyclic G) (hH : IsCyclic H) :
  IsCyclic (G × H) ↔ (card G).Coprime (card H)
reasoning: The Lean signature takes two finite cyclic groups G and H (via [Fintype G], [Fintype H], [Group G], [Group H], hG : IsCyclic G, hH : IsCyclic H), which corresponds to "G_1 and G_2 cyclic of orders m and n" since finiteness is implicit in having an order m, n. The conclusion `IsCyclic (G × H) ↔ (card G).Coprime (card H)` matches "G_1 × G_2 is cyclic iff m and n are relatively prime", with card G = m and card H = n. The quantifier structure (universal over such groups) is preserved by the type-level binders. No extraneous hypotheses or missing hypotheses; this is the standard faithful encoding.
